publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-01-11 18:28:26 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Photographs, negatives, contact prints, photographic enlargements, 16mm film
did.abstract: Photographs of Cornell University faculty and campus scenes taken by Barrett Gallagher include Alexander M. Drummond; University President Livingston Farrand and his wife, Daisy; University Presidents Deane W. Malott and Frank H.T. Rhodes; arts quadrangle buildings, museum, and other photographs for publications such as the Cornell Alumni News. Also, 16mm film Spring in Ithaca, ca. 1960.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Cornell University Class of 1936. Barrett Gallagher was a professional photographer and his work includes photographs of World War II action in the Pacific as well as a wide range of assignments for Fortune, Life, and other magazines. Gallagher also photographed politicians, musicians, and performers including Nelson Rockefeller, Carl Sagan, Ingrid Bergman, Leonard Bernstein, Mitch Miller, Burgess Meredith, Isaac Stern, Yehudi Menuhin, the John Kirby Sextet, Stuff Smith, and Cozy Cole.

arrangement.head: COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
arrangement.p: Except for Boxes 1, 2, and 44, whose contents are itemized, the notes on the contents of the boxes is not exhaustive but rather simply indicative of the types of photographs contained within.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series I. Prints, Cornell Related Boxes 1-2, 76, 118-119, 121, 164, 166
Series II. Motion Picture Film Box 3
Series VII. Color Transparencies, Tear Sheets and Magazine Covers Boxes 171-174

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Publication of photographs requires a credit line for Barrett Gallagher.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Barrett L. Gallagher photographs and film, #3956. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Photographs, negatives, contact prints, and selected enlargements; photographs of Cornell University faculty and campus scenes taken by Barrett Gallagher. Subjects include Alexander M. Drummond; University President Livingston Farrand and his wife, Daisy; University Presidents Deane W. Malott and Frank H.T. Rhodes; and arts quadrangle buildings. Also, 16mm film "Spring in Ithaca," ca. 1960.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Includes "Witness to War The Photographs of Edward Steichen and His Associates: An Exhibition," catalog of the exhibition held by the National Archives - Mid Atlantic Region, 1994.

dao.xlink:title: Movie Film - Springtime In Ithaca [Staff access]

daodesc.p: Movie Film - Springtime In Ithaca [Staff access]

Main lecture room, Electrical Engineering, Phillips Hall
Model of Puerto Rico Radio, Physics and Space Center. White shirt, Prof. William H. Erickson, Asst. Director, School of Electrical Engineering In coat, Research Assoc. H.J. Eckelmann.
Prof. William H. Erickson, Assistant Director, School of Electrical Engineering
Engineering Physics, ultrahigh vacuum electron diffraction camera for surface study of copper oxidation

Cornell Dramatic Club, University Theatre, Willard Straight
Ornithology lab, Sapsucker Woods. Arthur A. Allen, Professor Emeritus, honorary director (shirtsleeves). Professor Paul P. Kellogg, Ornithology (black coat, white hair), the bird-recording experts.
Professor Henry A. Detweiler, Assistant Dean, Architecture. Popular slide lecture on European Architecture for non-architects.
Professor Hans A. Bethe, Physics, in office
Professor Hans A. Bethe, Physics, in synchrotron - Newman Lab

Heptagonal Track Meet, Barton Hall. Annual event, only a remote campus can attract sufficient crowds

Vice President Theodore Wright, now retired
Collyer Boat House, Cayuga Lake inlet
Cornell University Medical College - Dr. Pap (George Papanicolaou) with his wife and assistants

Dr. E.C. Richardson, Surgery. Sophomore lab on retina

Medical College, Dr. Vincent duVigneaud, Biochemistry (Nobel Prize)
Plastic Surgery, Dr. Herbert Conway with Residents completing ten years of graduate study
Cornell College-The New York Hospital-Cornell Medical Center, New York City
Willard Straight Hall, the Student Union
Managing editor of Cornell Daily Sun pulls proof at 2 A.M. The student-run Sun is Ithaca's only morning paper.
